programme.desc: Odessa Carter (Whoopi Goldberg) needs to get to work as a nanny in the home of the affluent Miriam Thompson (Sissy Spacek), but she refuses to take the bus. Odessa is participating in the Montgomery bus boycott, protesting against the inequality between blacks and whites, so Miriam decides to offer Odessa a ride to work every day. Though the community and Miriam's husband (Dwight Schultz) insist she not get involved, the relationship between Miriam and Odessa has already changed for the better.

programme.title: The Legend Of Zorro

programme.title: 30 Minutes Or Less
programme.desc: In the action-comedy 30 Minutes or Less, Nick (Jesse Eisenberg) is a small town pizza delivery guy whose mundane life collides with the big plans of two wanna-be criminal masterminds (Danny McBride and Nick Swardson). The volatile duo kidnaps Nick and forces him to rob a bank. With mere hours to pull off the impossible task, Nick enlists the help of his ex-best friend, Chet (Aziz Ansari). As the clock ticks, the two must deal with the police, hired assassins, flamethrowers, and their own tumultuous relationship.

The daughter sends for Kid Shelleen, the hero of several legends, only the gunfighter has become a drunkard, but she teams up with him anyway to right the unjust murder of her father.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Chickadee Returns" - Jethro thinks he's in love with Chickadee Laverne, a stripper he met at the bank. She and the Clampetts have meet and have different ideas about what an "engagement" is, while Miss Hathaway tries to avert this disaster.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jethro's First Love" - Jethro decides that he wants to go a-courtin', so after Uncle Jed gives him a little education, he finds himself a woman--who happens to be a stripper. As usual, neither she or the Clampetts actually understand what the other is really saying.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampette Look" - Cynthia Fenwick makes her filthy rich mother dress in the "Clampett Look" when they visit the Clampetts, since she thinks Elly is a fashion maven. That only reinforces the Clampett's belief that the Fenwicks are poor.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Elly Starts to School" - Mrs. Drysdale recommends Elly for a high-brow finishing school in hopes that humiliating her will drive the Clampetts away; but instead Miss Hathaway convinces the girls at the school that Elly is a fashion trend-setter. Elly invites the filthy rich Fenwicks over, thinking they're destitute.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Granny's Garden" - Grannie has everyone up at the crack of down to plow the front yard for her garden, while Mr. Drysdale, Miss Hathaway, and a stubborn mule try to stop her.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Hair-Raising Holiday" - After Granny cuts off the top of Drysdale and Clyburn's (his doctor) hair for some homegrown remedy, Clyburn tries to keep her from ever doctoring again. Meanwhile, the Clampetts are looking forward to the Possum Day parade, which of course isn't celebrated in Beverly Hills, so Drysdale has to figure out how to have one to keep them happy.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies

programme.title: Raiders of Red Gap
programme.desc: Bob's Playin' A Mystery Hand... He's Marked For Death By Killer Bullets As Gunfire Echoes Over The Plains! After one of his ranch hands is killed when a gang attacks his ranch, Jim Roberts joins forces with his neighbor in an effort to fight off the Bennett Cattle Co., a company looking to rob them of their land. Things get increasingly dangerous as the company hires Butch Crane, a professional killer, to stop them. Directed by Sam Newfield (Racing Luck (1935), Go-Get-Em, Haines (1936)), Raiders of Red Gap stars Robert Livingston (The Three Mesquiteers (1936), Larceny on the Air (1937)) as Rocky (Lone Rider) Cameron and Al St. John (Billy the Kid Trapped (1942), Border Feud (1947)) as Fuzzy Q. Jones.

programme.title: Rage at Dawn
programme.desc: Randolph Scott plays James Barlow, an agent from Chicago hired to bring the notorious Reno brothers to justice. But wouldnt you know it? The Reno gang includes a beautiful young woman (Mala Powers), who catches Barlows eye. This western from director Tim Whelan (The Thief of Bagdad) features cinematography by two-time Oscar winner Ray Rennahan (Gone With The Wind), while the screenplay was by noted author Horace McCoy (They Shoot Horses, Dont They?). Theres also an all-star cast, including Forrest Tucker, J. Carrol Naish, Edgar Buchanan, Denver Pyle, and several other shaggy sidewinders!

programme.title: Colorado Kid
programme.desc: Cowboy star Bob Steele (Valley of Hunted Men, The Trail Blazers) headlines this Poverty Row western drama. As the Colorado Kid, he's falsely arrested for murder and has to clear himself. The colorful cast includes Ernie Adams, Frank LaRue, and Horace Murphy, with direction by Sam Newfield (The Terror of Tiny Town, The Mad Monster).

programme.title: Neath Canadian Skies
programme.desc: It's the mounties versus claim jumpers in this drama from Golden Gate Pictures. Based on an action-packed novel by James Oliver Curwood and directed by B. Reeves Maynard (Undersea Kingdom, The Phantom Empire), the film features an outstanding cast of Poverty Row regulars, including Russell Hayden, Inez Cooper, Cliff Nazarro, I. Stanford Jolley, and Kermit Maynard. For movie buffs: Cinematographer Marcel Le Picard began his career in the silent era and finished with exactly 200 credits!

programme.title: Red Ryder: Roll, Thunder, Roll!

programme.title: Strongman Champions League
programme.desc: Set in the beautiful mountain resort Zlatibor, the fifth stage of the Strongman Champions League 2017 sees 14 athletes from across the world competing for the gold medal of SCL Serbia 2017. The event welcomes top-athlete - and odds-on favourite - Matjaz Belsak (Slovenia), who has presented himself in an impressive shape so far. Can his opponents defeat him?

programme.title: Strongman Champions League
programme.desc: The next two stages take place in Quebec. With eight events over two days, SCL Canada is a true ‘heavyweight’. The athletes compete in: Truck Pull, Deadlift with cars, Log Lift, and the Atlas Stones. The World’s Strongest Viking Jean-Francois Caron from Canada is a favourite, as well as current SCL leader Matjaz Belsak (Slovenia) and promising underdog Jimmy Paquet (Canada).

programme.title: Strongman Champions League
programme.desc: Welcome to the second day in Quebec. The athletes are fighting hard to win this two-day event. The last points of this SCL stage can be won over four outstanding disciplines: Power Stairs, Dumbbell Press, Keg Toss, and finally, the legendary Farmer’s Walk. Who will leave Canada with the yellow ‘Leader Shirt’?

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly embraces "sweater weather" by making a savory Butternut Squash, Spinach, and Blue Cheese Pie. While he cooks, the hosts discuss expensive clothing habits and the woes of tight pants. A cooking question about pie layers hilariously derails into a conversation about dating terminology, and Michael reveals he is passing the Thanksgiving hosting torch to his son. The episode concludes with Michael giving away a lucky golf putter.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: It's Friday dessert day, and Carla Hall is making a comforting Peach Cobbler Pound Cake mashup. While Carla bakes, Michael Symon annoys Clinton Kelly with a deafening hand mixer. The trio discusses their favorite childhood holiday cookies, passionately debates whether black-and-white cookies are actually good, and shares ideas for crazy food mashups. The episode ends with a giveaway of a custom-designed apron from Carla.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall takes the lead in the kitchen as she introduces a playful and unexpected dish: fried Kool-Aid, inspired by nostalgic state fair treats from her childhood. As she walks through the process of making the vibrant red dough using Kool-Aid powder, flour, sugar, and a leavening agent, Clinton Kelly reacts with skepticism while Michael Symon shares his own less-than-great experience trying the dish at a fair. The conversation quickly turns humorous as Carla recounts a bizarre childhood memory of having a runny nose at the fair, prompting playful teasing from the group. As the dough is fried into donut hole-like bites, the hosts discuss technique, including how the dough naturally flips in hot oil and how to tell when it’s perfectly cooked. Carla elevates the dish by tossing the warm bites in lemon sugar and offering a Kool-Aid glaze for dipping, balancing sweetness with a bright citrus note. Once tasted, both Clinton and Michael are pleasantly surprised, praising the light texture and more refined flavor compared to traditional fair versions. The episode transitions into a lively fan mail segment where the hosts debate their least favorite vegetables and share personal preferences, followed by a special “fan of the day” moment celebrating a crew member’s mother’s birthday with jokes and a spontaneous song from Clinton. The episode wraps with the hosts encouraging viewers to engage with the show and teasing future fun.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly takes on a true first in his cooking career by attempting homemade corn dogs without any prior test run, bringing a mix of nerves and excitement to the kitchen as Michael Symon and Carla Hall cheer him on. Inspired by a popular recipe from Natasha’s Kitchen, Clinton walks through the batter and frying process in real time, navigating hot oil, technique adjustments, and plenty of playful banter. As the trio discusses their favorite fair foods—from funnel cake to childhood carnival memories—the conversation veers into hilarious territory, including rollercoaster mishaps and food-related regrets. Despite the pressure, Clinton successfully executes the corn dogs, achieving a golden, airy batter that impresses both Carla and Michael. The tasting confirms the dish as a win, with praise for its balanced sweetness, texture, and classic flavor. The episode also features a “Fan of the Day” segment, celebrating Charlene from New York with a personalized shoutout, along with a quick fan mail discussion about music preferences while cooking, revealing everything from Judy Garland to Metallica and R.E.M. The episode wraps on a high note, with Clinton’s confidence restored, the group celebrating a successful first attempt, and the hosts encouraging viewers to engage, subscribe, and bring the fun into their own kitchens.

programme.title: Simply Symons
programme.desc: In the debut episode of Simply Symons, Michael Symon welcomes viewers into his Venice, California front yard alongside his wife Liz Symon and their friend Olivia Annacone Cisic for an unfiltered, laid-back cookout. Celebrating Cinco de Mayo, Michael prepares simple yet flavorful black cod fish tacos on a plancha, walking through his approach to seasoning, cooking fish by sight, and building a bright, crunchy slaw with cabbage, citrus, and charred poblanos. The conversation flows easily as the trio shares cooking tips, debates ingredients like corn versus flour tortillas, and jokes about knife sharpening habits, cilantro preferences, and home cooking realities. Liz complements the meal with a refreshing jalapeño margarita, balancing heat with fresh lime juice, simple syrup, and a hint of orange liqueur, while adding her own creative twist with lemon and bitters. Throughout the episode, the group emphasizes flexibility in cooking—encouraging viewers to adjust flavors, garnishes, and spice levels to their own tastes. Lighthearted banter continues as they assemble tacos, discuss plating ideas, and highlight optional additions like crema and avocado. The episode wraps with a taste test, enthusiastic reactions to both the tacos and cocktail, and a reminder that great food doesn’t have to be complicated—just fresh, simple, and made with joy.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: The hosts celebrate their 25th show milestone with guest Chadwick Boyd, who elevates the classic Green Bean Casserole with caramelized onions, fresh beans, and a gruyere drop biscuit topping. While the casserole bakes, the crew debates mashed potato techniques, and Michael shares a hilarious story about how an AI misread his pan wall photo. A fan wins a Caraway pan set.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: A Fan Fridge rescue mission unfolds when a hypothetical Thanksgiving goes wrong. Michael Symon saves the day using Tammy's fridge ingredients to create a savory skillet of chicken thighs, Italian sausage, and sweet peppers. Meanwhile, the hosts share their current TV binges, Michael explains the meanings behind his tattoos, and Clinton sings "Brandy" for a fan. The episode wraps with a giveaway of Carla's measuring bowls.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Guest Danielle Sepsy of The Hungry Gnome joins the kitchen to bake her famous, plush Honey Corn Muffins. As the muffins bake, Clinton and Danielle test Carla and Michael's Long Island accents in a hilarious roleplay game. They also play Chew It or Screw It, share Thanksgiving food disaster stories, and give away a $75 gift card to Danielle's bakery to a fan in Virginia.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Kicking off Thanksgiving week, Clinton Kelly prepares a seasonal Delicata Squash Salad with goat cheese and pepitas. As the crew enjoys the autumn flavors, they discuss Thanksgiving versus Friendsgiving, dive into a comprehensive timeline for holiday prep, and give away a prize to a lucky fan from Oregon.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: It's Thanksgiving Eve, and Carla Hall takes the stress out of holiday cooking with her fast and flavorful Turkey Scallopini topped with a Cranberry Gastrique. The hosts share their best organizational tips, including Michael's famous oven grid and platter sticky notes. The trio also swaps hilarious stories about cooking under pressure and celebrity encounters.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: It's the Friday after Thanksgiving, and Chewed Up is all about transforming leftovers into brand-new masterpieces. Carla Hall whips up a decadent Ice Cream Pecan Pie using leftover pie slices, while Michael Symon cooks a savory Stuffing Frittata. The hosts confess their favorite late-night leftover habits, debate mayonnaise brands, and share wild stories about flying with pets.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: In this Fan Fridge episode, Michael Symon battles a stubborn blender to create a Whipped Cottage Cheese Dip with crispy mortadella chips. The crew embraces the pre-holiday scramble, sharing funny stories about morning commutes and paper cuts.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: During "holiday reset week," Clinton Kelly bakes a festive Stromboli Wreath in a bundt pan. The hosts discuss their vastly different childhoods, play "Holiday Do or Don't," and Michael shares a hilariously horrifying mix-up involving the Hotel Chelsea in New York.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Special guest Liz Symon joins the kitchen to bake her foolproof Orange Olive Oil Cake. While she bakes, Liz and Michael laugh off a viral AI rumor that they divorced, reveal their morning Wordle routines, and recall a disastrous early date involving bad crab stuffed shells.

programme.title: Recipe Lab with Chris Ying
programme.desc: "1:1 Pancake" - Spurred on by demand for a simple pancake recipe, Chris attempts to create an foolproof, impossible-to-forget pancake recipe.

programme.title: Recipe Lab with Chris Ying
programme.desc: "Burger Crunch Wrap" - Once again social media compels our man in the Lab to make something he’s never considered making: a bacon cheeseburger crunch wrap.

programme.title: Recipe Lab with Chris Ying
programme.desc: "Garlic Quesadilla" - Against his will, Chris uses copious amounts of butter and garlic to recreate a TikTok viral take on a quesadilla.

programme.title: Recipe Lab with Chris Ying
programme.desc: "Jungle Bird and Compari Sun" - It’s been a long week in the studio. Chris wraps up his night by demonstrating the only two cocktail recipes he knows: the Jungle Bird and the Campari Sun.

programme.title: Recipe Club
programme.desc: "Chris Ying Makes Slow Cooker Carne Asada" - Taking inspiration from Bricia Lopez's method for making her LA-style carne asada, Chris tries to make it in a slow cooker instead of on a grill. He uses a chuck roast and lets it cook low and slow in the marinade, coming away with something similar to Carne en su Jugo ("meat in its own juices"), but nonetheless delicious and very easy!

programme.title: Recipe Lab with Chris Ying
programme.desc: "Fluff Bread" - According to TikTok, egg whites + sugar + cornstarch + chocolate chips = cloud (a.k.a.) fluff bread. In this episode, Chris checks the math on this equation.

programme.title: Recipe Lab with Chris Ying
programme.desc: "One Pot 'Chicken Korma'" - Twitter is very upset about this Tasty UK recipe for one-pot chicken korma. Chris is 100 percent sure that 1 this is an intentional troll recipe; and 2 hes going to get canceled for testing it.

programme.title: Recipe Club
programme.desc: "Cocktail Weenies in Grape Jelly & BBQ Sauce" - Dave Chang, Chris Ying, and John deBary tackle a recipe straight out of the 1970s: Lit’l Smokies cocktail weenies simmered in barbecue sauce and grape jelly. We made the original PLUS three variations, including an ultra-luxe version, a keto version, and a one-pot version.

programme.title: Recipe Club
programme.desc: "Sogan Dolma (Bosnian Stuffed Onions)" - Chris, Dave, and Rachel take a beautiful, traditional dish of beef-and-rice-stuffed onions braised in tomato sauce, and see how far they can push it.

programme.title: Recipe Club
programme.desc: "Spaghetti with Sardines and Cherry Tomatoes" - Rachel Khong joins David Chang and Chris Ying for this episode focused on using tinned fish as the key ingredient. The recipe: Bill Pearis’s Spaghetti with Sardines and Cherry Tomatoes. Dave makes a keto-friendly version. Rachel tries to make it using ingredients from the pantry. Chris makes his vegan.

programme.title: Recipe Club
programme.desc: "Pork Belly Jae-Yuk Orzo" - Priya Krishna joins David Chang and Chris Ying to stir up some Korean-Italian magic with Ruth Lee’s Jaeyuk Pork Belly Orzo. Using the key ingredient of canned tomatoes, this is a suprisingly simple, hacky recipe that is delicious and comforting. Priya makes a keto-friendly version. Dave makes his using only shelf-stable ingredients. Chris makes it in under 20 minutes.

programme.title: Recipe Club
programme.desc: "Easy Boozy Piña Colada Poke Cake" - This one's a boozy doozy! Chris, Dave, and Bryan tackle Easy Boozy Pina Colada Poke Cake and see if they can make it for under twenty dollars, over two hundred dollars, or with only shelf-stable ingredients in a microwave.

programme.title: Recipe Club
programme.desc: "Mom's Crab Cakes" - Dave and Chris are joined by Rachel Khong to riff on the Dunn family’s tattoo-worthy crab cake recipe.

programme.desc: Gathering with family and friends around the table or for a game of boules is the life’s greatest pleasure for Jacques. He makes chicken wings, cucumber soup, merguez sausage, zucchini canapes, hummus and his father’s Fromage Fort. Simple food that can be made ahead means that you can enjoy a glass of wine with your company.

programme.title: Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home
programme.desc: Jacques shows that with a well-stocked pantry and a few good cooking techniques, you can make a tasty dish or a simple meal. His pantry essentials include potato flakes for a simple soup, spaghetti for a quick main dish, lentils, canned salmon, polenta and finally, in the freezer, a poundcake that can be transformed into a celebration dessert.

programme.title: Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home
programme.desc: Jacues knows that most of us want to eat a healthy diet with lots of organic produce. Now so much is available at farmers markets and in grocery stores, you don’t have to spend a fortune if you chose what’s in season when it tastes the best. But for those times you’re in a hurry, it’s okay to grab prewashed, precut or frozen vegetables to make your life easier.

programme.title: Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home
programme.desc: Jacques suggests that mastering simple skills and techniques will make your life easier and when you’re comfortable in the kitchen, you’ll be more creative and avoid the trap of a quick fix with a frozen meal…or worse! He teaches you how to sharpen knives, cut and prepare vegetables and eggs and finally make a delicious gratin.

programme.title: Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home
programme.desc: Jacques enjoyed many new food trends. But on a cold damp night, he still craves classic dishes like onion soup or for a family dinner or an elegant meal, a roast chicken. For him, these dishes will never go out of fashion, just like the apple galette he's been making for decades.

programme.title: Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home
programme.desc: For Jacques, a rite of spring is to enjoy the salad his father made every year at this time… Spring is also when asparagus appears and he pairs it with grilled tuna. He cooks fresh peas as soon as they arrive in the market and Jacques, not wanting to waste anything, uses the pods to make a soup. He ends with his friend Ed’s Pasta Primavera…

programme.title: Jacques Pépin Cooking at Home
programme.desc: For Jacques, a rite of spring is to enjoy the salad his father made every year at this time… Spring is also when asparagus appears and he pairs it with grilled tuna. He cooks fresh peas as soon as they arrive in the market and Jacques, not wanting to waste anything, uses the pods to make a soup. He ends with his friend Ed’s Pasta Primavera…

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: The old ladies selling their baskets full of vegetables and cheese on the Dolac market in Zagreb bring us back in time. At the market, we go from the breathtaking Mediterranean islands to the Austrian Alps, from freshly caught hake and simple Baltic snacks to internationally awarded cheese and a unique Turkey breed.

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: The pink city hosts a coffer full of world-famous food treasures. The Victor Hugo Market has black truffles of Quercy, Rocamadour cheese, foie gras, and white beans of the most traditional Cassoulet on sale. And Michelin-starred chef Michel Sarràn gives us his tips on what the belly of Toulouse really is.

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: Lying in the culinary heart of Vienna like an island, a little world of treats rises in the midst: the Naschmarkt food market. A throng of buyers and curious onlookers meander through the stalls all consumed by the scent of spices, distracted by the smell of sausage, and stunned by the diversity of turnips, tubers, foliage, and fruit on display. The Naschmarkt truly is Vienna's most popular belly.

programme.title: Exploring Europe's Great Food Markets
programme.desc: Food markets have a strong tradition in the Netherlands and many citizens count on their long street markets for their fresh food supplies. One of the oldest and most famous markets of the Netherlands is the Albert Cuyp Markt, a street market stretching along the upcoming (and "hip") neighborhood of de Pijp.

programme.title: Discovering the World
programme.desc: Muscat, the Horn of Africa, Sultans, the incense road, the straits of Hormuz, the Indian Ocean, and the "desert of deserts" are words and phrases that excite the imagination and are synonymous with legends of the past. Oman has successfully preserved its treasures of authenticity.

programme.title: The Silk Road
programme.desc: Some used to say Samarkand in central Uzbekistan was the center of the world. Follow the ancient "Royal Road" to the city associated with the great Turco-Mongol warrior Timur, visit his mausoleum, and learn about the imposing public square of Registan with the help of a local historian.

programme.title: The Silk Road
programme.desc: The modernity of life in Uzbekistan's capital of Tashkent is readily apparent, as is the incredible mix of ethnic groups that exist here. Visit a silk factory, a must for anyone retracing the Silk Road, then travel the age-old route through the mountains to Kyrgyzstan, the land of the nomads.

programme.title: The Toque 12

programme.title: The Toque 12

programme.title: Grannies on Safari

programme.title: Cattle King
programme.desc: Cattle rancher Sam Brassfield (Robert Taylor) engages in a war of wills with power-hungry Clay Mathews (Robert Middleton), whose allies include the brother of Brassfield's betrothed. In trying to muscle out the competition, Mathews frames Brassfield for damage to another rancher's property, nearly getting both men killed. But instead they join forces and enlist the help of President Chester A. Arthur (Larry Gates), leading to a shift in power and tension that can be settled only with bullets.

programme.title: Best of the Badmen
programme.desc: After the North defeats the South, Union Maj. Jeff Clanton heads to Missouri to provide the Confederacy's Quantrill's Raiders a chance to claim allegiance to the Union, thereby clearing their wanted status. But standing in Clanton's way are the corrupt lawmen Joad and Fowler, who would rather keep the men outlaws to collect the reward on their heads. After Joad and Fowler frame Clanton for murder, he manages to escape, becoming an outlaw himself.

programme.title: Dawn at Socorro

programme.title: The Westerner
programme.desc: Accused of stealing horses, drifter Cole Harden (Gary Cooper) finds himself before the infamous Judge Roy Bean (Walter Brennan). Aware of the judge's affection for actress Lily Langtry (Lilian Bond), Cole claims to know her and thus receives a suspended sentence. Soon after, Cole falls for an attractive homesteader (Doris Davenport) whose father (Fred Stone) is indirectly killed by Bean's henchmen. Looking for revenge, Cole tracks the judge to a theater where Langtry is set to perform.

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown
programme.desc: Weather has always been here, from long before arrived, and it will stick around long after we are gone. This unstoppable force has both enhanced and ruined people's days since the dawn of time...but it also creates some incredible situations. Lucky for you, we've captured some of the best for you to see. Strap in and prepare yourself, because it's time once again for Extreme Weather Countdown.

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown
programme.desc: Here at Extreme Weather Countdown, no natural phenomenon is too crazy, and we've returned with an episode chock-full of footage from the eye of the storm. Settle in, because there are rough seas ahead.

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown

programme.desc: Are you ready to be taken on the ride of a lifetime as you journey through sandstorms, thunderstorms, and snow storms? I sure hope so, because that's what's in store here on another episode of Extreme Weather Countdown!

programme.title: Extreme Weather Countdown

programme.title: Where Is Baby? - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Peekaboo! Sing along with this adorable song for kids, “Where Is Baby?” Where is baby? Where is baby? Where is baby? There he is.

programme.title: Finny's First Day Of School - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Finny is a bit nervous on the first day of school, but Myrtle the Turtle is even more anxious and literally hides her shell! Surely Myrtle will come out of her shell when their teacher, a whale named Mr. Magellan, has them draw and present their favorite things to the class! Nope. Myrtle is even more nervous than ever! Being a good friend, Finny helps Myrtle through her anxiety, and the tiny turtle finally shows her drawing to everyone.

programme.title: Baby Shark - Finny The Shark and more
programme.desc: Baby Shark, Mama Shark, Papa Shark, Grandma Shark, and Grandpa Shark are out for a family swim and Baby Shark tries and tries to catch the little fishies but his skills aren’t yet up to the task. Just when he thinks he finally has them a (very big) friend of the little fishies steps in to save the day!

programme.title: Dharma's Digger - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Oh no! While digging deep at a top secret construction site, Dhama’s digger gets splashed with smelly sludge! Everyone says that Carl's Car Wash is the place to get all cleaned up, so Dharma skedaddles over to see how Carl can help. With a quick mess assessment and a few twists of some knobs, Carl’s suds-sational car wash is ready to give Dharma’s vehicle a good scrub-a-dub! Dharma rolls out one happy customer, and ready to get back to work on the site that Carl is itching to see!

programme.title: Mr. Golden Sun - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Mister Sun, Sun, Mr. Golden Sun...please shine down on me! It’s the Mr. Sun song from Super Simple Songs!

programme.title: Finny's First Sleepover - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: It’s Finny’s first sleepover and Finny is nervous to sleep at Myrtle the Turtle’s house. Throughout the night, Finny becomes more and more comfortable and after a spooky story, Finny becomes the bravest of everyone at the sleepover! Finny can't wait to do a sleepover again – but this time, at Finny’s house!

programme.title: When I Grow Up - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: What do you want to be when you grow up? Maybe a firefighter? Or an astronaut? Or maybe a dancer? Practice talking about jobs and careers with this super simple and super fun song, “When I Grow Up”.

programme.title: Enchanted Ice Cream - Super Simple
programme.desc: Today, the Bumble Nums are going to make Enchanted Ice Cream Sundaes! With a map in hand, Humble, Grumble, and Stumble search the orchard for the secret ingredient … and discover a fairy treehouse leading to an enchanted world! The Bumble Nums travel through the portal…but do they find the ingredient they’re looking for? And can they get it back to the kitchen in time for the Cooking Countdown? Watch and see!

programme.title: Hello Hello! - Super Simple Puppets and more
programme.desc: Sing and dance along with the Super Simple Puppets to “Hello Hello!”! It’s a classic Super Simple action song for kids.

programme.title: Down By The Bay - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Did you ever see cat wearing a hat? How about a goat rowing a boat? Well you can in this video for the classic children’s song, Down By The Bay!

programme.title: Erupting Raspberry Lava Cake - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: The Bumble Nums are making an Erupting Raspberry Lava Cake but the secret ingredient is at the top of Mount Fruity Volcano! Can Humble, Grumble, and Stumble climb the volcano and get what they need before it blows? And can they do it in time for the Cooking Countdown? Watch and see!

programme.title: Finny The Shark - Down In The Bay and more
programme.desc: Did you ever see a shark glowing in the dark? Did you ever see a stingray dancing at the ballet? Finny and Mama Shark are singing a rhyming song about all of their silly friends down in the bay! Sing along!

programme.title: Let's Go For A Drive! - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Get in on the fun and join Milo in his pretend car for a quick ride. Do you see the ducks crossing the road? How about people walking on the sidewalk? Wait, don't cross on a red light!

programme.title: Mr. Pig & Mr. Mouse Have A Mess On Their Hands - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Mr. Mouse and Mr. Pig love making food and having their friends over to eat it. But afterwards, their house is a complete mess! It's so gross, they don't even want to drive their van home to clean up. How can Mr. Mouse and Mr. Pig share their love of food with their friends without cluttering their home? Mr. Monkey has a tasty idea! What is it? Watch and find out!

programme.title: The Pinocchio - Sing Along With Tobee - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Caitie made a fluffy bird marionette named Fifi! Fifi is full of jokes and Tobee can't stop laughing! Caitie knows another marionette who is just as silly as Fifi. It's Pinocchio! There's even a whole song about him called "The Pinocchio." After Caitie and Tobee sing and dance to the song, Fifi can't help but tell one last joke. And guess what? It quacks Tobee up!

programme.title: 10 Little Dinosaurs - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: 10 Stegosauruses! 10 Triceratopses! 10 Pterodactyls! 10 Brontosauruses! And 10 Tyrannosaurus Rex! Wow! This Super Simple counting song introduces the numbers 1-10 and also some very cool dinosaur names! Can you say them all?!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: For Children's Day, Wang Min's Dad has invited his son and his classmates to visit his toy factory. While cleaning up before the visit, a robot toy goes on a rampage. The Super Pets encounter an itchy lion at a sanctuary in South Africa. Jett and Astra deliver a back scratcher, but the lion's itchiness is stronger than they realized!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets go into a video game arcade and play a video game called Dessert Kingdom. They are watched by a boy named Yoonho and they decide to go into the game itself. When the Super Pets unearth a woolly mammoth, a Nenets boy wants to keep it. The mammoth must be able to pull a sleigh so Jett and Dizzy deliver a mammoth size sleigh.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: When a T-Rex skeleton on display in a Copenhagen museum is brought to life, a boy orders a remote controller to summon a giant robot T-Rex that he built to help calm it. Jett and the others arrive in the Jurassic Period to return Tyranno to her proper place in time. A volcano erupts throwing fireballs everywhere and causing a dinosaur stampede.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets spend a day at a Pet Hotel in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. The daughter of the hotel owner gets help from the Super Wings to keep them well behaved. After the Jet Pets' Mini Base collides with a small comet, Jett and Astra rush a space scanner to a young boy and together and the three search space for the small 'Baba' comet.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: It's Jett's birthday and almost all of his friends are throwing him a big birthday party! Sky sends Donnie, Paul, and Dizzy out on missions and nobody is left at Jett's party. When the truck transporting race cars gets lost, Jett, Jerome and Dizzy show up in Monza, Italy to take part in a very special Super Wings race against Golden Boy.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: While in Thailand, Jett Pet and Paul Pet's fruit is swiped by a monkey. Thanawat asks for help getting his toys back so they create an LED ball with a tracking system. The Super Pets travel to Madrid, Spain where they help a boy secure a case for his pet tarantula to ride the train. However an accident causes his tarantula to become super-sized!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: After eating Super Pets' trick chocolates, Jett can only move very slowly. An emergency call comes in from a young girl in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, who needs an ice maker delivered. When the Super Pets discover an erupting underwater volcano, a girl orders a megaphone to alert all the villagers fishing in the water. However, they don't listen to her warnings.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets are on World Aircraft to help Jett give some kids a tour. After Storm demonstrates how the package printer works, two packages get mixed up. The Super Pets travel to Tokyo, Japan where they help the Paper Rangers repair an Origami Robot for an action movie. Afterward, they get asked to be ACTORS in the movie!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets encounter mermaid students learning to befriend fish in Cebu, Philippines. Jett and Jerome deliver a microphone and speaker to a girl who wants to sing with them. The Super Pets meet a girl in Paris whose greatest wish is to go to the top of the Eiffel Tower. The elevator cannot run due to an expected storm so Jett and Donnie deliver a tram.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Storm and Stella search for Jett and Donnie after the two Super Wings disappear in thin air. Astra locates their whereabouts - they're in Paris, 1889! While exploring Osaka, Japan, the Super Pets accidentally rip a boy's koinobori for the Children's Day Festival. Jett and Dizzy deliver a huge koinobori for him.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets meet a boy and his giant robot on Jeju Island in South Korea. Before a typhoon comes, Taehoon needs to help his father harvest all the tangerines on their farm. When a little girl and her Dad accidentally fall in an Incan secret dark room, Jett, and Ellie show up in Machu Picchu, Peru, to take part in a historical rescue mission.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: When the Super Pets arrive at Ha Long, Vietnam, they meet a boy who wants to take his pet snail on a walk but the snail is too slow. So the boy asks for a snail size skateboard. The Super Pets go to Germany where they participate in a water fight. They accidentally shoot Mona's necklace. Jett and Astra deliver a magnifying glass to find the missing bead.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: When the Super Pets accidentally break a boulder on the roof of a house in Monsanto, Portugal, a girl orders tape to stick it back together. Golden Boy tries to outshine them. The Super Pets' Mini Base accidentally gets tangled up in an octopus kite, pulling it to the ground and destroying it. The Super Wings deliver paper and string to make a new kite.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Marty, with their new electric powers, save an amusement park in Germany. Jett and Dizzy also rescue a boat, an eel, and fix a jaguar chase, making everyone happy.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: In Singapore, Jett and Lucie use electric powers to shrink Happy the giant robot dog. In Paris, Jett and Marty turn a giant baguette into a train to deliver it to a festival.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Traver time-travel to uncover a dinosaur footprint’s creator. Jett and Dizzy rescue a bus stuck in a ditch using Monster Truck Tires, getting the kids to school on time.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett, Tiki, and Taki recover stolen costumes to save a dragon dance in Sichuan. In Antalya, Jett and Marty protect a giant sandcastle after Golden Girl's magic enlarges it and Jett

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Tino retrieve a mood lamp from mammoths after a Stone Age detour, delivering it to LA. In Cairo, Jett and Sally restore order after Golden Girl's magic animates the Sphinx.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Take your little explorer on an exotic musical expedition to meet wild animals from across the globe. Journey to the jungle, savannah, desert and forest.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Learn about the numbers 1,2,3,4 & 5 through rhyme, real-life footage, counting, stories, and repetition - accompanied by classical music and real world imagery.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Get moving with Baby Einstein! Real, live-action footage and classical melodies engage young viewers as they discover simple words related to movement. Enjoy encouraging melodies and live-action footage of babies and animals as you learn and move alongside your favorite Baby Einstein friends. Dance along as you watch real, live action footage of animals, children, and toys.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As babies grow and change, so does the landscape around them. From fiery red leaves to falling snowflakes; from flowers in bloom to fireworks in July, every season of the year has its own special magic just waiting to be discovered.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As babies develop, their understanding of the world, and how its pieces "fit" together, continues to progress. DISCOVERING SHAPES playfully introduces five basic forms circle, oval, triangle, square and rectangle as each appears in the context of toys, nature and everyday objects.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: At 12 months, babies can identify specific sounds, and they re also starting to match the sounds they hear with the objects that make them. Meet the Orchestra -- First Instruments presents your little "maestro" with a playful introduction to the sights and sounds of a real orchestra -- from the clashing of cymbals to the purring of the cello and everything in between!

programme.title: Baby Einstein Ocean Explorers
programme.desc: Cal, Neptune, Opus, Dean, Tinker and others learn about the flora and fauna of the ocean through STEAM-based principles and real-life footage.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Share a rainbow of sights and sounds with your baby as you discover the wonders of color together! Join puppet host "Vincent Van Goat" as he creates six Van Gogh masterpieces exploring red, green, blue, yellow, orange, and purple. World Of Colors is a spirited celebration of creative expression.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As babies turn one year old, they continue to develop their communication skills. Meanwhile, with you as their guide, they also begin to explore the world beyond their front door. Baby's Favorite Places presents them with a playful introduction to 20 words for the names of familiar locations from the market, to the library, to the local park.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Meet fascinating creatures from across the globe and learn to run, hop, and crawl like your favorite animal friends. Dance and sing along to discover animal names and the native habitats of the world's most unique creatures. See real-life images of lions and zebras on the African savannah, then learn animal names through repetition and narration, or simply watch your favorite animals play!

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: With the Dolittle, and other Baby programs under their belts, the tots who follow Julie Aigner-Clark's Baby Einstein series will sidle up to Baby Newton expecting a similar exploration of building blocks to brilliance, but this time there's a wrinkle or two. Animation makes its first appearance, and parents steeped in keep-it-simple culture may object to the springy "I Know My Shapes" song, a departure from classical-only video predecessors.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Meet fascinating creatures from across the globe and learn to run, hop, and crawl like your favorite animal friends. Dance and sing along to discover animal names and the native habitats of the world's most unique creatures. See real-life images of lions and zebras on the African savannah, then learn animal names through repetition and narration, or simply watch your favorite animals play!

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Discover the wonderful world of water and deep dive into all the amazing creatures that call the ocean home. Learn simple words about the sea like water, coral reef, and ocean through narration and repetition. Engaging live-action footage of your favorite ocean friends and classical melodies will dazzle your little explorer as they experience the magical world under the sea.

programme.title: Super Supremes Dinosaur Song & More Kids Songs
programme.desc: The kids are so excited to learn about dinosaurs. Here is a collection of our best videos.

programme.title: Super Car Royce - Back To Back
programme.desc: Watch this compilation from Super Car Royce Back To School!

programme.title: Little Red Car - Popular Songs
programme.desc: Watch this whole bunch of popular songs by the Little Red Car for kids. Hope you enjoy them!

programme.title: Humpty Dumpty Nursery Rhymes and Kids Songs - Farmees
programme.desc: Enjoy Farmees Humpty Dumpty and popular easy to learn nursery rhymes for babies and toddlers, which the children love to sing and dance along with. Learn these kids songs and new words at the same time.

programme.title: Luke and Lily Baby Shark - Shark Song For Kids
programme.desc: Kids! We've taken your favorite songs like Baby Shark, The Muffin Man, Little Bo Peep and put them in a compilation for several minutes of pure, uninterrupted fun learning and singing with Luke and Lily.

programme.title: Super Supremes Old Macdonald & More Kids Songs
programme.desc: Kids! Are you ready to go round the Farmees town? Hop on-board this Red Wheels On The Bus to enjoy your fun learning ride.

programme.title: Daddy's Red Car + Fun Nursery Rhymes for Kindergarten
programme.desc: Kids songs and nursery rhymes that your little one will love! Come join Super Supremes as they sing and dance in this fun kids video. Learn preschool songs, numbers, abc, shapes, colors and more made easy and fun with this nursery rhyme and kids song playlist with rhymes like Daddy’s red car, I spy wheels on the bus, Twinkle Twinkle Little Star and more.

programme.title: Humpty Dumpy & More Kids Rhymes (Little Treehouse)
programme.desc: Do you kids know what happened to Humpty Dumpty when he sat on the wall? Learn this classic nursery rhyme with the Little Tree House gang, plus many other fun songs and dances.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: The Hedgehogs find out that Sonia has inherited a castle. She basks in the idea of having a castle for headquarters.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: A group of Freedom Fighters is obliterated by a swarm of metallic fly-bots. When Manic goes looking for the insects, he is stung with serum.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: A speed enhancing fluid deemed Sonic Tonic is created by Robotnik to make him as fast as Sonic so that he may finally catch him.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: The Hedgehogs congregate on the Floating Island in their latest effort to find their mother. But Sleet and Dingo also have an interest in this island.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: As Sonic begins to chow chili dogs for a snack, he finds a message in the sack from his mother urging him to head to Speedster Island.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Sonic becomes aggravated that maybe their efforts of fighting for freedom seem pointless. Sonia suggests that they go to the Oracle for advice.

programme.title: Sonic Underground
programme.desc: Sonic, Manic and Sonia find an abandoned baby in an alley and begin to look for the baby's parents. Manic becomes very fond of the child.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic attempts a solo rescue to prevent prisoners from being roboti-sized and is hit by a Memory Scrambler that erases short-term memory.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: After a disastrous failed mission, Sonic laments that they can't go back and prevent Robotnik's take-over. Dulcy tells them they can and takes Sonic and Sally to the Floating Island where they find the "Time Stones".

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: In Part II, Sonic and Sally steal the Roboti-sizer plans, stop the Destroyer from polluting the Great Forest and save Little Sonic, Little Sally and Dulcy's mother, Sabina.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Antoine decides to study martial arts but after he wrecks most of Knothole Sonic sends him off to practice in the woods. //Tails begins his training to become a real Freedom Fighter by learning tracking skills while on a camping trip.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: After Sonic is almost sucked inside the Void, he finds a huge Ring which Sally believes is an ancient relic but it turns out to be trick of the Sorcerer, Nagus. Sally and Bunnie are drawn inside the Void and when Sonic comes to rescue them.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When a blizzard hits Knothole causing Dulcy to demolish a few huts during a landing, Sonic is forced to bunk with Antoine. // During a rainstorm, Rotor is putting the finishing touches on his new female robot assistant, named Ro-Becca.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: When Uncle Chuck's "bug" reveals Robotnik's search for the legendary Deep Power Stones, he informs Sally, who tries to retrieve data regarding the Royal Family Secret. But she's denied access via a time-encoded lock-out.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: After Antoine saves Sally's life during a raid, Sonic can't stand his bragging and zips in to see Uncle Chuck, who finds out his "bug" in Robotnik's HQ is malfunctioning.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: When Ruby and Choco arrive at the village, they find a very worried looking Ling Ling. Gina, Ellie, and Thunderbell went camping in Rainbow Forest but they saw a monster in the forest, got scared and had to run away. // Ruby is struggling to make a doll with her dad. Ruby and Choco get their suitcase and as Ruby swings her umbrella, there they are, in front of Rainbow fountain. Mayor Ling Ling tells her that today is the day of Princess Kiki's ballet performance.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ruby is looking at shoes that are now too small for her but she is unwilling to throw away. Ling Ling is worried about Thunderbell. His scooter broke so he is not able to carry out his work as a mail carrier. // Gina calls Rainbow Ruby because today is the day to show their appreciation to Mayor Ling Ling. It took a long time for the village friends to prepare an amazing painting for the mayor. Unfortunately, Thunderbell accidentally destroyed the painting while delivering it.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: There is a new menu at the restaurant, which is very popular. There is a long line in front of the restaurant but there weren't enough staff members to serve the villagers the food. Rainbow Ruby turns into a waitress using her magic suitcase and serve tables kindly. // Rainbow Village is full of marbles. The marbles that Rainbow Ruby spilled in her room poured down into the village from up above the sky! Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to transform into a paramedic.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Gina, Thunderbell, and Choco try to cheer Jessy up but their efforts are of no use. While talking to Mayor Ling Ling, Rainbow Ruby realizes that today is the day that Jessy first arrived in Rainbow Village! Jessy was upset that her friends forgot such a meaningful day to her. // Ling Ling has caught a bad cold and was sneezing all over the place. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase and transforms into a doctor and cares for him.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Ling Ling is worried about the play planned for tonight. There are no costumes ready for the villagers so it's hard to figure out which roles they're playing. Rainbow Ruby transforms into a costume designer and designs costumes for her friends. // It is very windy in Rainbow village and Paige, a paper doll, is flying to wherever the wind takes her. She is so light that she is blown away by the wind. Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase to become a fire fighter and rescue Paige.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby

Thunderbell, Ellie, and Gina are trying to pick them up and clear the field but it is not easy since the pokey pods are so spikey. // Ling Ling tells Rainbow Ruby that he lost his glasses. Then Ellie tells her she lost her needles, Gina her hat, and Thunderbell his wheels. Who could have taken away all these things?

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Detective BJ is on the case when several sports balls suddenly disappear. Determined to find the mysterious ball thief, BJ falsely accuses Riff of being the thief. BJ learns he cannot jump to conclusions and must find the whole story to see who the thief really is.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Celebrating her birthday in the park with friends, Layla is more interested in hot dogs than her mother's Middle Eastern food. When Barney reads the story of Aladdin and the Magic Lamp, Layla is transported to a Middle Eastern marketplace where she learns different cultures have many exciting foods and customs to offer.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Captain Pickles and Wonder Princess both wanted to be the super-est superheroes. When Captain Pickles said he could jump over the moon, everyone wanted to see. BJ knew he couldn't really jump that high, but he could jump high enough to save Emma's balloon. Everyone was proud of him, even if he couldn't jump over the moon. BJ was a hero.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney comes up with the idea to have a sports day in the park. All day, they play games like soccer and hopscotch among others, all for fun. Meanwhile, Baby Bop meets a girl with special needs named Jill, as well as her mom. As Baby Bop gets to know Jill more, she encourages her to play hopscotch with the others. In the end, Baby Bop and Jill win the trophy for hopscotch and she and her mother leave.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: After the kids judge a spider by its creepy looks, Barney tells them the story of The Reluctant Dragon where a shepherd became friends with a dragon, who was judged by her appearance.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop's friend Monty the butterfly passes through the park with a flock of friends on their way to Mexico. When Monty gets left behind, Barney takes everyone on a visit to Mexico to return Monty to his insect friends and family and give Baby Bop the chance to say goodbye to her butterfly friend.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney's excited to find out that Rusty's family is adopting a new baby sister from China; however, Rusty's not sure if he's going to be a good brother, and he doesn't know much about China. Barney shows Rusty and the others some info China on the computer and BJ and Baby Bop talk about siblings. Through Barney's Travel Book, Barney takes Rusty, BJ and Baby Bop on an adventure to China where he can learn more about his new sister's heritage. When they get there, the people are celebrating Chinese New Year. From lion dances and talented performers to tasty treats, making lanterns and puppet shows, the celebration never runs out of fun. By the time he returns to the park, Rusty excitedly meets his new baby sister, named Mei Ling, and realizes he will be the best big brother ever.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Celebrate the things that make us special! Barney and his friends discover that trying something new can be fun. Jackson treats everyone to his healthy but unusual snack, while Baby Bop has a special picnic for BJ consisting mostly of her favorite foods.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Oh baby! Oh baby! David has a new baby brother and is now a big brother. But David doesnt know what a big brother is supposed to do and isnt sure hes ready. Barney and his friends help David learn about new babies and how to be a big brother.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney and his friends keep on truckin'! BJ enjoys helping with his tow truck, while Barney shows everyone what it's like to be behind the wheel of a real truck. When Jackson's special truck loses a tire, Barney wheels in to the rescue!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: After seeing some big construction machines roll by, Barney and his friends are inspired! With a little imagination, they build a castle made out of boxes. By exploring the ways things are built, and the big machines and tools used to build them, Barney builds in the fun!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Exercise is good for everybody and every body! From running to touching toes to jumping jacks to some favorite songs and games, Barney and his friends grow strong through various activities and having fun with exercise.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: It's fun and games with Barney and his friends! BJ's favorite way of getting exercise is by playing sports and games. When Baby Bop is shy about trying new games, Barney and his friends show her how tee-rific trying something new can be.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Nick shows Barney and the rest of the kids his number cards that he and his mom had put together. He doesn't really think they're fun at first. But Barney helps his friends discover just how fun numbers can be by taking them on a number hunt in the park. With Barney and his friends, you can always count on a fun day!

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Who is buried under Glasgow Cathedral and why? Who is Saint Mungo, and why is he so big? Why does Glasgow City Chambers look like a palace? How did this seaport town become a center for Scottish Enlightenment? And how did the creativity of one man, Charles Rennie Mackintosh, leave a permanent mark on this city?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why did the English choose Jamestown, Yorktown and Williamsburg, Virginia as their early American settlements? Why did King William III and Queen Mary II establish a university here? What was life like for the colonists here? What crazy concoctions are to be found in a colonial apothecary?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: After nearly 500 years, French is still the official language of this North American city. How has this UNESCO World Heritage Site retained its Frenchness after all this time? Why does the Chteau Frontenac hotel look like a French castle, and what does it have to do with the Canadian railway? Whats so curious about Rue Saint-Jean, Place Royal and Petit-Champlain?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Englands Great West Way follows the ancient path from London to Bristol. We take the train & get curious at stops along the way: Stonehenge, Baths masonic architecture, the Roman Baths, the S.S. Great Britain, sample some curious pub pies, learn some cockney rhyming slang, and visit some charming villages too.

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why is La Grande-Place so gilded? How did Brussels chocolate legacy begin? Where can you walk through the pages of a comic book? How did Flanders become such a source of Master artists?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why is there a giant wall in the middle of Cardiff? Who built Cardiff Castle? And Coch Castle? Why does Cardiff have so many Victorian and Georgian arcades? What is a bara brith? And laverbread? (Seriously, what is it?). Where did Princess Diana give her first public speech in Welsh?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: How did Manchester kick-start Railway Mania in the Industrial Age? What is Manchesters Water Palace? What is so curious about the architecture of the John Rylands and Chethams Libraries? Why is a bee the symbol of Manchester? Which pub owner shooed her patrons out with a broom? Why is the National Football Museum in Manchester?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: How did Hong Kong get its name? What is so junk-y about a junk boat? Get curious about the Big Buddha, the Po Lin Monastery, and the Wisdom Path on Lantau Island. Then, the Kung Wo Tofu Factory, to learn the centuries-old tofu-making technique. We also take you inside a Tin Hau Temple, the Man Mo Temple, and a traditional Qipao dress-making shop.

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: We get curious about the stunning natural landscape of Wales. We visit Brecon Beacons National Park, Pembrokeshire National Park, St. Davids (the smallest city in Britain), Hay-onWye (WalesTown of Books), the Victorian seaside resort town of Tenby, and lots and lots of curious castles.
